controls.name: Webhook signing secret configured
controls.description: Verifies that a webhook signing secret is configured when webhooks are enabled.
controls.why_it_matters: Without a signing secret, any actor with knowledge of your webhook URL can send arbitrary commands to your OpenClaw installation.

check.prerequisite_skip_reason: Webhooks not enabled — check not applicable
check.condition: findings does NOT contain checkId matching signing-secret-related pattern

controls.remediation: Set the webhook signing secret environment variable before starting the gateway. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-001
controls.false_positive_notes: None expected on enabled-webhook installs — this is a binary configuration check.

controls.name: Sandbox mode appropriate for deployment context
controls.description: Checks that sandbox mode is appropriately configured when multi-user heuristics are detected.
controls.why_it_matters: Running without sandbox in a multi-user context allows any user to execute arbitrary code on the host system through prompt injection.

check.prerequisite_skip_reason: Single-user deployment — sandbox check not applicable
check.condition: security.trust_model.multi_user_heuristic checkId present indicates sandbox concern

controls.evidence_template: Multi-user heuristic detected: {present}. Sandbox mode concern flagged.
controls.remediation: If users may be mutually untrusted, split trust boundaries or set agents.defaults.sandbox.mode="all". See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-002
controls.false_positive_notes: Single-user personal-assistant deployments will not trigger this — only multi-user heuristic scenarios.

controls.name: No ineffective deny command entries
controls.description: Checks that gateway.nodes.denyCommands entries are all valid, recognized command names.
controls.why_it_matters: Ineffective deny entries give a false sense of security — commands you think are blocked may still be executable.
controls.remediation: Use exact command names in gateway.nodes.denyCommands. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-003
controls.false_positive_notes: No known false positives — OpenClaw validates command names against its own registry.

controls.name: No open (unauthenticated) groups
controls.description: Checks that no messaging groups are configured with open (unauthenticated) access alongside elevated or runtime tools.
controls.why_it_matters: Open groups with elevated tools allow any member to trigger high-impact commands via prompt injection.

check.condition: findings does NOT contain checkId 'security.exposure.open_groups_with_elevated' or 'security.exposure.open_groups_with_runtime_or_fs'

controls.remediation: Set groupPolicy="allowlist" and tighten elevated tool allowlists. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-004
controls.false_positive_notes: May fire on intentionally open demo/test groups — use exclusions for known-safe configurations.

controls.name: Elevated tools usage acknowledged
controls.description: Checks whether elevated tools are enabled and prompts user acknowledgement.
controls.why_it_matters: Elevated tools grant broad system access. While expected for personal-assistant deployments, operators should consciously acknowledge this configuration.
controls.remediation: If intentional, run 'clawvitals exclude NC-OC-005 reason "personal assistant setup"' to acknowledge. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-005
controls.false_positive_notes: Expected on personal-assistant deployments. The acknowledgement prompt prevents this from being a recurring noise finding.

controls.name: Workspace file access scoped
controls.description: Checks that filesystem access is scoped to workspace when multi-user heuristics are detected.
controls.why_it_matters: Unrestricted filesystem access in a multi-user context allows any user to read/write arbitrary files on the host.

check.prerequisite_skip_reason: Single-user deployment — workspace scope check not applicable

controls.remediation: Set tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true for multi-user deployments. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-006
controls.false_positive_notes: Only fires when multi-user heuristic is active. Single-user deployments are unaffected.

controls.name: Dependency integrity verifiable
controls.description: Checks that the dependency integrity status is verifiable (not a known failure state).
controls.why_it_matters: Unverifiable dependency integrity means supply-chain attacks could go undetected.

check.condition: update.deps.status is not a known failure value

controls.remediation: Ensure a valid lockfile exists for your package manager. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-007
controls.false_positive_notes: Standard pnpm installs may show 'unknown' status due to missing lockfiles — this is expected and does not indicate a problem.

controls.name: All configured channels healthy
controls.description: Checks that all configured messaging channels have successful probe results.
controls.why_it_matters: Unhealthy channels mean security alerts and scan results may not be delivered, leaving operators blind to issues.
controls.remediation: Check channel configuration and connectivity. Run 'openclaw health' for details. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-008
controls.false_positive_notes: Channels configured but intentionally unused may show as unhealthy. Use exclusions if this is expected.

controls.name: OpenClaw update available
controls.description: Informational notice when a newer OpenClaw version is available in the registry.
controls.why_it_matters: Staying current ensures you have the latest security fixes and features.
controls.remediation: Run 'openclaw update' to install the latest version. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-OC-009
controls.false_positive_notes: None — this is a factual check against the registry.

controls.name: Reverse proxy trust correctly configured
controls.description: Checks that trusted proxies are configured when the gateway binds to loopback.
controls.why_it_matters: Without trusted proxy configuration, client IP headers can be spoofed, bypassing local-client security checks.
controls.remediation: Set gateway.trustedProxies to your proxy IPs or keep the Control UI local-only. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-AUTH-001
controls.false_positive_notes: Expected on loopback-only installs that don't use a reverse proxy. Consider exclusion if intentional.

controls.name: No API tokens in workspace files
controls.description: Scans workspace files for exposed API tokens and credentials.
controls.why_it_matters: API tokens in workspace files can be accidentally committed to version control or accessed by unauthorized users.

check.condition: No token-like patterns found in workspace files

controls.remediation: Move API tokens to environment variables or a secrets manager. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-AUTH-002
controls.false_positive_notes: May match non-token strings that look like tokens. Higher false-positive risk than authoritative checks.

controls.name: No tokens in OpenClaw log files
controls.description: Scans OpenClaw log files for accidentally logged credentials.
controls.why_it_matters: Tokens in log files persist on disk and may be exposed through log aggregation or shared access.

check.condition: No token-like patterns found in log files

controls.remediation: Configure log redaction for sensitive fields. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-AUTH-003
controls.false_positive_notes: May match non-token strings in logs. Higher false-positive risk than authoritative checks.

controls.name: OpenClaw is behind latest release
controls.description: Checks whether the installed OpenClaw version is behind the latest available release.
controls.why_it_matters: Running older versions may miss important security patches and bug fixes.
controls.remediation: Run 'openclaw update' to install the latest version. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-VERS-001
controls.false_positive_notes: None — this is a factual check against the registry.

controls.name: OpenClaw not more than 2 minor versions behind
controls.description: Checks that the installed version is within 2 minor versions of the latest release.
controls.why_it_matters: Being significantly behind increases the risk of missing critical security fixes and API incompatibilities.
controls.remediation: Run 'openclaw update' to install the latest version. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-VERS-002
controls.false_positive_notes: None — based on factual version comparison.

controls.name: Node.js runtime within LTS support window
controls.description: Checks that the Node.js runtime is within the LTS support window.
controls.why_it_matters: Running on unsupported Node.js versions means no security patches for the runtime itself.

check.condition: Node.js version is within active LTS support window

controls.remediation: Upgrade to a supported Node.js LTS version. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-VERS-004
controls.false_positive_notes: None expected — based on official Node.js release schedule.

controls.name: No deprecated API usage warnings
controls.description: Checks for deprecation warnings in OpenClaw security audit output.
controls.why_it_matters: Deprecated APIs may be removed in future versions, potentially breaking functionality or exposing security gaps.
controls.remediation: Review and update usage of deprecated APIs. See: https://clawvitals.io/docs/NC-VERS-005
controls.false_positive_notes: Uncertain data source — may not produce results on current OpenClaw versions.
